$comment: Machine-readable catalog index for the explainer skill. The skill reads this to route a request to technique(s) + fidelity, then copies the matching template from assets/catalog/ and swaps its data (see assets/catalog/_data.template.js). Do not duplicate prose here that lives in references/*.md.
$comment_weak: '.when'/'.weak' are load-bearing for the post-build fit-check (references/quality-gate.md 'Fit-check'). '.when' = the question this technique answers; '.weak' = questions it does NOT. The fit-check quotes these verbatim to convict a suboptimal pick or spot a catalog gap. Keep them precise. New techniques enter first as candidates in references/catalog-gaps.md, then get promoted here on review.

ascii.what: Compact text diagram inline in chat. No file.
ascii.default_for: small subject, quick 'explain this', review-comment context

mermaid.default_for: medium subject, anything shipped into a doc/PR/wiki

html.what: Standalone interactive explainer using the locked shared design system.
html.default_for: large/complex subject, or when it must be viewed from multiple angles

families.value: where data comes from and goes

techniques.when: see where a change sits in the end-to-end flow; onboarding to a path
answer 'where does this value come from / go?' WITH the code at each point
techniques.weak: control flow; timing; the shape of the change itself

techniques.when: understand execution order and what happens on a run
techniques.weak: static structure; comparing many values at once

techniques.when: compare two MODES of one structure (before/after, prod/test, sync/async)
let the reader choose depth; docs serving both skimmers and diggers
call ORDER and who-calls-whom; message-level differences
'what calls what' / call hierarchy; navigating a path like an outline
literate line-by-line walkthrough of real code; teaching a function
techniques.weak: architecture overview; flow across many files
techniques.note: PRIMITIVE: embed inside other techniques wherever code is shown

techniques.when: domains that ARE a state machine; transitions + trigger conditions
techniques.note: SIDE-CAR: rides alongside a step/flow view well

techniques.when: keep global context while reading local detail in a large change
build intuition for ORDER and causality by sweeping back/forth
techniques.note: often redundant with runtime-step; prefer step unless continuous scrubbing genuinely helps

techniques.when: depth-on-demand without EVER losing the whole
techniques.weak: precise A/B diff; printed/static output; simple linear reading

techniques.when: portable data-flow / control-flow that embeds in a PR, wiki, or ticket
techniques.weak: interactivity; deep per-node code; runtime state

techniques.when: portable call-sequence (who calls whom, in order) for a doc/PR
portable state machine for a doc/PR
classic node-edge DFD: sources, stores, processes, sinks; the 'good old' diagram

combined_examples.$comment: Composition patterns. See references/decision-rules.md for the when-to-combine rule. Golden reference lives in references/example-dryrun/catalog/combined-examples/.

A.name: Spine + Annotated Source
A.when: big-picture map that also lets you read the real code in place

B.name: Step-Through + State-Machine side-car
B.when: watch execution AND the state path it drives, together

C.name: Fisheye + Annotated + Minimap
C.when: deep-read one part while the whole stays on screen (never lose context)

D.name: Altitude + Annotated + Provenance
D.when: choose your depth, then read code AND trace values at the bottom

E.name: The Workbench
E.when: ANTI-PATTERN reference only: shows where integrated tips into cluttered; do not ship by default
